criteria.name: Problem Definition
criteria.description: Student clearly defines the problem and identifies key elements of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The problem definition is concise, well-structured, and directly related to model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The problem definition is vague, incomplete, or unrelated to model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Relevant Concepts
criteria.description: Student identifies relevant concepts and techniques in model evaluation and overfitting, such as cross-validation and regularization.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student identifies a range of relevant concepts and techniques, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student fails to identify key concepts and techniques or demonstrates a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Evaluation Metrics
criteria.description: Student selects appropriate evaluation metrics for the problem, such as accuracy, precision, and recall.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student selects a range of relevant evaluation metrics, demonstrating an understanding of their strengths and limitations.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student selects inappropriate or incomplete evaluation metrics, demonstrating a limited understanding of their use in model evaluation.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Student accurately identifies errors and gaps in AI output, such as overfitting or underfitting.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student consistently identifies errors and gaps in AI output, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student fails to identify errors and gaps in AI output or demonstrates a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Analysis of Results
criteria.description: Student analyzes AI output and results, considering factors such as bias, variance, and model complexity.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student provides a detailed analysis of AI output and results, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student provides a limited or superficial analysis of AI output and results, demonstrating a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Model Evaluation
criteria.description: Student evaluates the performance of the model, considering factors such as accuracy, precision, and recall.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student provides a comprehensive evaluation of the model's performance, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student provides a limited or incomplete evaluation of the model's performance, demonstrating a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

typical_errors: Failure to consider the impact of hyperparameter tuning

criteria.name: Correction Commands
criteria.description: Student provides clear and effective correction commands to the AI, such as adjusting hyperparameters or selecting alternative models.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student provides concise and well-structured correction commands, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student provides vague or ineffective correction commands, demonstrating a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Model Selection
criteria.description: Student selects an appropriate model or algorithm, considering factors such as model complexity and data characteristics.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student selects a suitable model or algorithm, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student selects an inappropriate model or algorithm, demonstrating a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

criteria.name: Hyperparameter Tuning
criteria.description: Student tunes hyperparameters effectively, considering factors such as learning rate and regularization strength.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student tunes hyperparameters effectively, demonstrating a thorough understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student fails to tune hyperparameters effectively, demonstrating a limited understanding of model evaluation and overfitting.

typical_errors: Failure to consider the impact of regularization on model performance
